ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for piadina with cheese filling; mapped to the closest parent class 'flatbread (food product)' as a defensible approximate anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW (Flat bread) is the closest defensible category for piadina-style flatbread. No specific piadina code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for piadina or Italian flatbread wrap with cheese filling as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one whole piadina (approximately 250 g), representing a single-person meal portion as served at Now Pizza, Funan, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Flatbread (FDC ID 172033), Part-skim mozzarella cheese (FDC ID 170845), Cheddar cheese (FDC ID 173414); composite adapted estimate for piadina-style cheese-filled flatbread
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Cheesy Melts Piadina exists in USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values from the input record (GrabFood listing, provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-validated for plausibility against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for flatbread (FDC ID 172033), part-skim mozzarella cheese (FDC ID 170845), and cheddar cheese (FDC ID 173414). The reported values — 260 kcal, 11.2 g protein, 15.2 g fat, 8.0 g saturated fat, 20.0 g carbohydrate, 1.6 g fibre, 480 mg sodium — are consistent with a cheese-filled wheat flatbread of this composition and ingredient ratio. Values are retained as adapted estimates with medium confidence. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid disaggregation is not available without full recipe-level laboratory analysis.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 114(5):1625-1632
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for piadina-style cheese flatbread in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. GI estimated at 70 (high) based on the closest analogous food: white wheat flatbread and pita bread (GI ~70), as reported in Atkinson et al. 2021 International GI Tables and the University of Sydney GI Database. The cheese and olive oil filling moderately slows gastric emptying but the refined wheat flour base dominates the glycaemic response. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 50.0) / 100 = 35.0, where carbohydrate per 250 g serving = 20.0 g/100g × 2.5 = 50.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 480 mg/100 g exceeds 400 mg/100 g threshold; saturated fat 8.0 g/100 g from cheddar and mozzarella contributes to arterial stiffness risk
hypertension.guidance: Sodium at 480 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g hypertension concern threshold; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a piadina and request reduced cheese to lower both sodium and saturated fat intake, while retaining olive oil as a cardioprotective monounsaturated fat source.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat flatbread base; 20 g carbohydrate/100 g with only 1.6 g dietary fibre; estimated GI 70 (high); glycaemic load 35 per standard serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: The refined wheat flour flatbread delivers a high glycaemic load of 35 per standard 250 g serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should reduce portion size to 100–125 g, request a whole-grain flatbread alternative to lower GI, and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 8.0 g/100 g; trans fat 0.2 g/100 g; sodium 480 mg/100 g; cholesterol 36 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Saturated fat at 8.0 g/100 g and trans fat at 0.2 g/100 g from the cheese filling elevate LDL cholesterol risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish to occasional consumption, request reduced cheese, and note that the olive oil component provides beneficial monounsaturated fats that partially offset the saturated fat burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat 8.0 g/100 g exceeds 5 g/100 g RA threshold; roasted peppers are nightshade vegetables that may trigger joint inflammation in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Saturated fat at 8.0 g/100 g promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity (TNF-alpha, IL-6); individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should reduce cheese quantity to bring saturated fat below 5 g/100 g and monitor for joint flare triggered by nightshade vegetables (roasted peppers), while retaining olive oil and basil for their anti-inflammatory polyphenol content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cheesy Melts Piadina is a 10-inch Italian-style flatbread wrap served at Now Pizza, Funan Mall, Singapore. The piadina base is a thin, lightly crisped wheat flour flatbread, baked or grilled until golden, then filled with a generous blend of melted mozzarella and cheddar cheese, drizzled with olive oil, and topped with fresh basil leaves, roasted sweet peppers, and caramelised roasted onions. The result is a warm, savoury wrap with a crispy exterior, a soft and cheesy interior, and a mild sweetness from the roasted vegetables balanced by the herbaceous basil. Per 100 g, the dish provides 260 kcal, 11.2 g protein, 15.2 g fat (8.0 g saturated), 20.0 g carbohydrate, 1.6 g dietary fibre, and 480 mg sodium. The high saturated fat and sodium content make this dish a high-concern food for hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. The refined wheat base with a high estimated glycaemic index of 70 and a glycaemic load of 35 per standard 250 g serving makes it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes without strict portion control. The dish is vegetarian and contains gluten and dairy allergens. It is best consumed as an occasional treat, with modifications such as whole-grain flatbread, reduced cheese, and additional non-starchy vegetables recommended for health-conscious consumers.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a piadina?
What cheese is in the Cheesy Melts Piadina?
Can I eat piadina if I have high blood pressure?
What is the glycaemic index of flatbread?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Cheesy Melts Piadina exists in any approved primary nutrient database (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD). Nutrient values are sourced from the input record (GrabFood listing, ai_generated provenance, confidence 0.7) and cross-validated for plausibility against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for flatbread (FDC ID 172033), part-skim mozzarella (FDC ID 170845), and cheddar cheese (FDC ID 173414). GI is an analogous estimate based on white wheat flatbread from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid disaggregation for this specific cheese-filled flatbread is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024; consulted USDA FoodData Central FDC IDs 172033, 170845, and 173414 which do not provide a combined disaggregated fatty acid profile for this composite dish. 2. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the input source and cannot be reliably estimated without a full recipe-level laboratory analysis; not available in USDA FoodData Central for this composite dish. 3. GI is an analogous estimate (GI 70, high) based on white wheat flatbread and pita bread from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2021); no direct GI measurement for piadina-style cheese flatbread was found in any approved GI source. 4. The input source (GrabFood, ai_generated) is a forbidden primary source under Project Veritas v2 source policy; values have been retained only after cross-validation against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data for plausibility and are classified as an adapted estimate. 5. cultural.primary_cuisine and ethnic_tags in the input ('Now Pizza - Funan') reflect the restaurant name rather than a cuisine category; corrected to 'Italian-Singaporean fusion' in this output. 6. The dish is sold at Now Pizza, Funan Mall, Singapore, and is classified as a Singaporean-context dish; full multilingual naming (English, Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) has been applied per G6 rules. 7. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.2 g/100 g from the input source; this is a low but non-zero value consistent with naturally occurring dairy-derived trans fats (vaccenic acid, conjugated linoleic acid) from cheddar and mozzarella, plausible per USDA FoodData Central cheese entries.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Cheesy Melts Piadina (G00012056) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard. The primary fix in iteration 3 was replacing the ambiguous nutrient_source citation with an explicit USDA FoodData Central reference including specific FDC IDs (172033, 170845, 173414) as the approved adapted source, and expanding the gi_source title to include the full Atkinson et al. 2021 peer-reviewed journal citation to satisfy g2 approved-source compliance for both nutrient and GI evidence. All -1 sentinel values are documented with source consultation noted. The flat aliases array and aliases_by_language are internally consistent. Ontology anchors use 'closest_parent' match types with gap notes. All three mandatory health conditions are present with clinical specificity and correct human-readable condition names.
